stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates continued community interest and practical application of Meta's Llama models in the AI ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, llama, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/AI ecosystem.

stories.title: Nvidia announces RTX Spark as ‘the most efficient PC chip ever built’
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Nvidia is expanding its role into general consumer PC chipmaking, moving beyond just GPUs.
The new chip family, starting with RTX Spark, will be integrated into the core of laptops and mini-PCs.
This marks a significant industry shift as Nvidia enters the space alongside Intel, AMD, and Apple.

stories.title: ChatGPT for Google Sheets exfiltrates workbooks
stories.author: hackerBanana
bullet_points: A security vulnerability exists where a ChatGPT integration for Google Sheets can exfiltrate user workbooks.
This highlights significant data security risks associated with third-party AI integrations in productivity tools.
The incident serves as a critical warning regarding the security implications of connecting sensitive data platforms to external AI services.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
The model is associated with the deepseek_v3 architecture and conversational capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for macOS.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating active development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to deploy new gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and come in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are made available under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: This is the Microsoft Surface Laptop Ultra with Nvidia RTX Spark
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Microsoft is re-entering the market with a new flagship portable device, the Surface Laptop Ultra.
The device features a new Arm-based Nvidia chip, signaling a renewed push into custom silicon.
This effort follows a previous, costly attempt by Microsoft to utilize an Arm-based Nvidia chip in the original Surface.

stories.title: RayDer: Scalable Self-Supervised Novel View Synthesis from Real-World Video
stories.author: Björn Ommer
bullet_points: Introduces RayDer, a unified, feed-forward transformer for self-supervised Novel View Synthesis (NVS).
Addresses the scalability challenges in NVS by consolidating camera estimation, scene reconstruction, and rendering into one backbone.
Transforms the complex NVS problem into a well-posed, single-model scaling problem, leveraging real-world video data.

stories.title: Vision-Language Models Suppress Female Representations Under Ambiguous Input
stories.author: Mahzarin R. Banaji
bullet_points: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) exhibit gender bias when presented with ambiguous input images.
Models tend to default to male representations for occupations when gender is not clearly visible in the input.
The research highlights a gap in current alignment techniques regarding how models handle real-world, ambiguous scenarios.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline associated with this model is text-generation, indicating its primary function.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, placing it within the mainstream NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added to enhance Claude Code's real-time information access.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: The QD-OLED gaming monitor that started it all got a big upgrade
stories.author: Cameron Faulkner
bullet_points: Alienware is set to unveil new gaming monitors at Computex 2026.
The announcement includes two new OLED options for the year.
They are debuting a successor to their original QD-OLED gaming monitor from 2022.

stories.title: Apple’s strategy for smart glasses is the same as smart watches
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Apple's approach to smart glasses mirrors its strategy for the Apple Watch.
The goal is not just to compete with rivals like Meta, but to disrupt the entire eyewear industry.
The strategy involves challenging established watch brands such as Swatch, Fossil, and Seiko.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The infrastructure supporting the botnet was reportedly linked to a Russia-based residential proxy network.
The story falls under cybersecurity, indicating ongoing threats from organized malicious actors.

stories.title: LongTraceRL: Learning Long-Context Reasoning from Search Agent Trajectories with Rubric Rewards
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: Addresses the core LLM challenge of long-context reasoning, where models struggle with extensive, distracting content.
Introduces LongTraceRL, a new Reinforcement Learning approach designed to improve reasoning over long trajectories.
Overcomes limitations in existing RL methods by providing verifiable rewards that supervise intermediate reasoning steps, rather than just final outcomes.

stories.title: Giving Sensors a Voice: Multimodal JEPA for Semantic Time-Series Embeddings
stories.author: Henrik Ohlsson
bullet_points: Introduces CHARM, a new model for general-purpose representation learning in heterogeneous multivariate time series.
CHARM integrates channel-level textual descriptions into a Transformer encoder, making it equivariant to channel order.
The model is trained using a Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA) with a novel loss function.

stories.title: Functional Attention: From Pairwise Affinities to Functional Correspondences
stories.author: Daniel Cremers
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of operator learning, which involves learning mappings between infinite-dimensional function spaces.
Critiques existing transformer-based methods for treating continuous fields as discrete tokens, thereby ignoring global functional structure.
Proposes 'Functional Attention,' a novel mechanism that reinterprets attention as a functional correspondence between adaptive bases.

stories.title: Positional versus Symbolic Attention Heads: Learning Dynamics, RoPE Geometry, and Length Generalization
stories.author: Cristobal Rojas
bullet_points: The research investigates the learning dynamics of attention heads in Transformer models (like GPT-J) to improve understanding of their behavior.
It compares positional reasoning (number tasks) against symbolic reasoning (letter tasks) within a controlled setting.
The study aims to enhance the predictability and safety of large language models when deployed in novel scenarios, particularly regarding length generalization.

stories.title: Language Models Learn Constructional Semantics, Not To Mention Syntax: Investigating LM Understanding of Paired-Focus Constructions
stories.author: Leonie Weissweiler
bullet_points: The research investigates whether large language models (LLMs) possess robust 'constructional semantics' for rare grammatical pairings.
It specifically tests understanding using Paired-Focus constructions in English, such as 'let alone' and 'much less'.
The study addresses the open question of whether open-source models can achieve this level of nuanced semantic understanding.

stories.title: LongTraceRL: Learning Long-Context Reasoning from Search Agent Trajectories with Rubric Rewards
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: Addresses the core LLM challenge of long-context reasoning, where models struggle with extensive, distracting information.
Introduces LongTraceRL, a new method leveraging Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) to improve context handling.
Overcomes limitations in existing RL methods by providing supervision for intermediate reasoning steps, rather than just final outcomes.

stories.title: What Gets Unmasked First? Trajectory Analysis of Diffusion Models for Graph-to-Text Generation
stories.author: Chengkai Li
bullet_points: Introduces a systematic study of Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) for graph-to-text generation.
Finds that MDLMs prioritize generating entities first, unlike linear autoregressive LLMs.
Identifies a new, undocumented failure mode in supervised fine-tuning of these models.

stories.title: What Am I Missing? Question-Answering as Hidden State Probing
stories.author: Xiaodan Zhu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the under-explored mechanisms of test-time reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs), particularly after the rise of chain-of-thought.
It proposes using question-asking as an inference-time intervention method.
This method aims to articulate and gain insight into the model's internal hidden state during processing.

stories.title: Semantic Triplet Restoration: A Novel Protocol for Hierarchical Table Understanding in Large Language Models
stories.author: Yuqi Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of table question answering in LLMs, where layout information is often lost or poorly represented.
Proposes 'Semantic Triplet Restoration (STR),' a novel protocol to rewrite cells as atomic semantic triplets.
Aims to overcome limitations of current methods (HTML/Markdown serialization) by reducing markup overhead and improving alignment inference.

stories.title: Preference-Aware Rubric Learning for Personalized Evaluation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Addresses the critical bottleneck of evaluating personalized alignment in evolving LLMs.
Highlights the inadequacy of current evaluation methods (automatic metrics, LLM-as-a-judge) for capturing subjective, user-specific preferences.
Proposes a novel approach: Preference-Aware Rubric Learning to better align models with individual user tastes.

stories.title: UniAudio-Token: Empowering Semantic Speech Tokenizers with General Audio Perception
stories.author: Xiao Zhou
bullet_points: UniAudio-Token addresses the 'acoustic blindness' in existing semantic speech tokenizers used by Audio-LLMs.
The framework aims to integrate general audio perception capabilities into semantic tokenizers without sacrificing speech performance.
It represents an advancement in making Audio-LLMs more versatile beyond purely speech-centric tasks.

stories.title: 1-Bit Bonsai Image 4B Image Generation for Local Devices
stories.author: modinfo
bullet_points: The story discusses a new image generation technique called '1-Bit Bonsai'.
The key feature is its capability for running image generation locally on devices.
This suggests a trend toward more efficient, on-device AI models.

stories.title: Unlawful by design: Exposing the human rights costs of generative AI
stories.author: amnesty.org via mccd
bullet_points: The article critiques generative AI, arguing it is inherently flawed regarding human rights.
It focuses on the ethical and human rights costs associated with the deployment of generative AI technologies.
The piece suggests a critical examination of the design principles underpinning current AI systems.

stories.title: I Let an AI Agent Hunt Open Source Bounties for 96 Hours — Here's the Brutal Truth About What Actually Works
stories.author: zeroknowledge0x
bullet_points: Explores the practical application of autonomous AI agents in security/bug bounty hunting.
Provides a 'brutal truth' assessment of the effectiveness of current AI agents in real-world tasks.
Focuses on the intersection of AI automation and open-source security practices.

stories.title: The AI Code Protection Landscape: 13 Products Compared
stories.author: genevieve_breton_cb795f52
bullet_points: The article provides a practical comparison of 13 tools designed to safeguard source code.
The core focus is on preventing sensitive data leakage when interacting with AI systems.
This addresses a growing and critical security concern in the AI development lifecycle.
